name: ◁ TOFU WRAPPED IN WILD BETEL LEAF

ingredients.item: 30 to 35 wild betel leaves (see Note), divided
ingredients.original: 30 to 35 wild betel leaves (see Note), divided

ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked and minced
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked and minced

ingredients.item: ½ ounce (14 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and minced
ingredients.original: ½ ounce (14 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and minced

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil, for brushing, plus more if needed
ingredients.original: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil, for brushing, plus more if needed

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon crushed roasted peanuts, for garnish
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon crushed roasted peanuts, for garnish

ingredients.item: ½ cup (120 ml) vegan fish dipping sauce, for serving
ingredients.original: ½ cup (120 ml) vegan fish dipping sauce, for serving

ingredients.item: 1. In a large bowl, mash tofu blocks with a fork into fine crumbles.
ingredients.original: 1. In a large bowl, mash tofu blocks with a fork into fine crumbles.

ingredients.item: 2. Cut 5 wild betel leaves into fine strips and add to the tofu, along with mushrooms, lemongrass, five-spice powder, salt, stock powder, and sugar. Mix well and set aside for 15 minutes.
ingredients.original: 2. Cut 5 wild betel leaves into fine strips and add to the tofu, along with mushrooms, lemongrass, five-spice powder, salt, stock powder, and sugar. Mix well and set aside for 15 minutes.

ingredients.item: 3. Place a wild betel leaf, shiny side down, on your work surface with the tip of the leaf at the top. Place 1 tablespoon of filling near the tip. Starting from the tip, roll tightly into a cigar shape. When you reach the stem end, use a toothpick to punch a hole in the middle of the roll. Insert the leaf stem into the hole to secure. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.
ingredients.original: 3. Place a wild betel leaf, shiny side down, on your work surface with the tip of the leaf at the top. Place 1 tablespoon of filling near the tip. Starting from the tip, roll tightly into a cigar shape. When you reach the stem end, use a toothpick to punch a hole in the middle of the roll. Insert the leaf stem into the hole to secure. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.

ingredients.item: 4. Brush the rolls all over with vegetable oil. Place them on grilling skewers, 5 rolls per skewer, working with one roll at a time, with the skewer going through the stem in the center.
ingredients.original: 4. Brush the rolls all over with vegetable oil. Place them on grilling skewers, 5 rolls per skewer, working with one roll at a time, with the skewer going through the stem in the center.

ingredients.item: 5. Grill on an outdoor charcoal grill until leaves start to wrinkle (about 10 minutes), flipping and brushing with more oil about halfway through. Alternatively, bake them in the oven at 350°F (175°C) until the leaves start to wrinkle (15 to 20 minutes, flipping and brushing with more oil about halfway through). You can also fry the rolls in 3 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat until the leaves start to wrinkle (2 to 3 minutes per side).
ingredients.original: 5. Grill on an outdoor charcoal grill until leaves start to wrinkle (about 10 minutes), flipping and brushing with more oil about halfway through. Alternatively, bake them in the oven at 350°F (175°C) until the leaves start to wrinkle (15 to 20 minutes, flipping and brushing with more oil about halfway through). You can also fry the rolls in 3 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat until the leaves start to wrinkle (2 to 3 minutes per side).

ingredients.item: 6. Arrange on a platter, garnish with crushed roasted peanuts, and serve with vegan fish dipping sauce.
ingredients.original: 6. Arrange on a platter, garnish with crushed roasted peanuts, and serve with vegan fish dipping sauce.
